mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal consumption expenditures directly increase corporate revenue growth expectations, compressing equity risk premiums and lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in RSAFS expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and signals restrictive financial conditions, which mechanically depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rate hike signal, or the signal's predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate indicate a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that boosts consumer spending power and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 50 basis points in annualized forward returns, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost inflation and supports corporate margin expansion, thereby generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds its 12-month moving average, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in housing starts signals robust consumer confidence and downstream construction demand, which directly lifts earnings expectations for cyclical S&P 500 sectors and produces positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return distribution shows no statistical difference between positive HOUST print months and negative print months (Welch's t-test p > 0.05), or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses below a 4.5 percent threshold, it confirms a labor market tightness that historically precedes wage-driven consumption growth, triggering a positive repricing of equity cash flows and lifting S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain flat or negative when UNRATE breaches the 4.5% threshold, or the signal's cumulative return curve underperforms a buy-and-hold benchmark by more than 15% over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of rising federal funds rates and accelerating core inflation compresses real yields and forces equity risk premium expansion, which mechanically drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative forward returns with >60% accuracy, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels, indicating the signal captures volatility rather than macro fundamentals.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth combined with steady employment expansion confirms a mid-cycle economic acceleration that validates higher earnings multiples, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as institutional capital rotates into growth sectors.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of observations, or the rolling 1-year Rank IC consistently falls below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.
